behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the lint rule’s local detection logic (template text in, violation out). No real harness wiring, filesystem, subprocess, or model behavior is required to trigger the core defect.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the lint function template strings that include known phase-instruction lines (and near-miss variants) from SKILL.md fixtures in-memory; assert it flags exact/normalized leakage and does not flag unrelated text.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation/fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears at module handoffs: template + interpolation + fixture injection + assembler output. Unit tests of the linter alone can miss leakage introduced during assembly.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-construction pipeline with representative fixtures, capture the final assembled prompt string, and assert leakage patterns are absent post-interpolation.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from the plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: This depends on local file loading plus invocation wiring; the defect is often incorrect source-of-truth wiring (hard-coded rules bypassing files), which is a multi-module local interaction problem.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real test invocation against a temporary plugin/skill directory, mutate skill file content, and assert the invoked prompt/context reflects file changes (and does not pass when hard-coded fallbacks are the only source).

behaviors.description: If the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails with the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: The intended guarantee is behavior quality from real skill context; pass/fail depends on actual model output changing when guidance degrades. Mocked/stubbed model responses can hide this regression.
behaviors.test_description: Run an A/B test with the same user-style prompt: (A) intact SKILL.md and (B) degraded/removed section, using the real model call path. Assert behavior checks pass for A and fail (or significantly degrade) for B across repeated runs with a stability threshold.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test explicitly requires real model variability handling, so lower-level mocks would not validate the claim.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering/side-effect contract across modules (model response handler, artifact writer, parser). The core risk is pipeline sequencing, not OS/external dependency correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real response-processing pipeline with a malformed output fixture; assert the raw artifact exists and contains unparsed text even when parsing fails, and verify write occurs before parser error propagation.

behaviors.description: Assertions verify structured behavior (for example field placement or schema shape), not mere keyword presence that could come from prompt echoing.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is typically in assertion wiring against parsed outputs: a weak keyword matcher can pass echoed text. Need parser + assertion modules together to catch false positives.
behaviors.test_description: Feed outputs that contain required keywords but incorrect schema/field placement through the real assertion path; assert failure. Feed correctly structured outputs without exact keyword echoes; assert pass.
